description:
While wind power has seen steady expansion since 2002, the sector entered 2025 with significant untapped potential and ambitious legislative backing. As a primary pillar of the national energy transition, wind energy is currently undergoing a strategic scale-up to meet the requirements of the Renewable Energy Expansion Act (EAG).
**Current Infrastructure and Performance**
By the end of 2024, Austria’s wind power landscape reached a mature stage of development, characterized by high operational reliability and a concentrated geographic footprint.
* Total Capacity: The nation’s installed wind capacity reached approximately 4,100 MW.
* Asset Base: This capacity is generated by over 1,400 individual wind turbines distributed across the country.
Grid Contribution: In 2024, wind energy successfully covered 13% of Austria’s total electricity demand, equivalent to a generation output of 9.3 TWh.
This information has been published initially on https://www.energie.gv.at
Installed wind power capacity in MWp, source: EAG Monitoring Report 2025 by E-Control via energie.gv.at, accessed on: February 26, 2026, 10:38 a.m.
title: Wind Capacity per Federal State
